## 2026-06-15
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — feat: extend fab memory-index --check with severity exit-code tiers and --json flag (#416)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — **`fab memory-index --check` gained severity exit-code tiers + a `--json` flag** (direct follow-on to 5ewp, mechanizing its prose compatibility detection). The `fab memory-index` Subcommands entry now documents the tiered `--check`: exit **0** clean / **1** benign drift (the former "out of date" condition — existing "non-zero = stale" consumers keep working) / **2** destructive loss, with three loss categories (curated description→`—`, tombstone row dropped, custom grouping flattened — the mechanical form of `/docs-reorg-memory`'s prose signals). Tier 2 enumerates losses to stderr by category and emits the `→ run /docs-reorg-memory to remediate ...` pointer (the orchestrator that relocates tombstone rows and dispatches `/docs-hydrate-memory` backfill mode for descriptions; backfill alone does not relocate tombstones). New optional `--json` (with `--check`) emits `{tier, drift, losses:[{category, path, detail}]}` (snake_case) on stdout, suppressing human text, exit code unchanged. Loss is a strict subset of drift (one render pass); a born-compatible fab-kit tree is provably never tier 2. The classifier + existing-index-row parser are pure functions in `internal/memoryindex` (cmd reuses the existing rendered-vs-existing byte-compare; tier-2 surfaced via in-handler `os.Exit(2)` mirroring `pane_capture`/`pane_send`, tier 1 via returned error → cobra exit 1, tier 0 nil). Consumers note updated: `/docs-reorg-memory` now derives compatibility findings via `--check --json` (older-binary prose fallback retained), and refuse-before-regen guards (exit 2) live at `/docs-hydrate-memory`, `/fab-continue` hydrate, and reorg — each a born-compatible no-op. CLI doc mirror is `_cli-fab.md` § fab memory-index. (glwc)
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — fix: Binary Truth-Telling, Error-Surfacing & Inference Correctness (#418)
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — **Scaffold write failures propagate (Go, fab-kit).** `lineEnsureMerge` (`src/go/fab-kit/internal/scaffold.go`, behind the `.envrc`/`.gitignore` fragment line-ensure merges) now returns its two previously-swallowed `os.WriteFile` errors up the `scaffoldTreeWalk` chain — a failed fragment write surfaces as a non-zero `fab sync` instead of a silent half-scaffold. The `scaffoldDirectories` doc comment that falsely claimed "Write failures are propagated" for a sibling that discarded them was corrected. Success-path behavior unchanged; only honest failure surfacing. Added a note to § Delegation Pattern. (jznd)

## 2026-06-13
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — fix: Offline-first `upgrade-repo` (default to systemVersion) (#409)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — **Offline-first `upgrade-repo` default.** No-arg `fab upgrade-repo` now resolves its target to the running binary's embedded `systemVersion` (offline), instead of calling the GitHub API for the newest release. A new `--latest` boolean flag opts into the old GitHub-API path; an explicit `<version>` arg still wins (and silently ignores `--latest`); a `dev`/unstamped binary falls back to the network. `Upgrade(systemVersion, targetVersion string)` gained a third `useLatest bool` param; the no-arg resolution block became a 3-arm precedence switch (`useLatest` → `LatestVersion()`; else `systemVersion != "" && != "dev"` → `systemVersion`; else → `LatestVersion()` fallback). `upgradeCmd()` (`cmd/fab-kit/main.go`) wires the `--latest` flag; the `LifecycleCommands` `Short` for `upgrade-repo` is now "Upgrade the repo's kit to the installed binary's version (or --latest / an explicit version)" (`internal/lifecycle.go`). Everything downstream of resolution is unchanged (short-circuit, `EnsureCached`, `runSync`, F18 stamp-after-sync, migration detection). 4 new behavioral tests + the 9 existing callers updated to the new signature (`upgrade_test.go`). `_cli-fab.md` gained an `### upgrade-repo Version Resolution` precedence table (constitution-mandated CLI doc). *Why*: the old API-default tripped GitHub's unauthenticated rate limit (60 req/hr/IP) and hard-failed with a misleading `HTTP 403` on shared hosts; the binary already knows its own version, so the dominant "match my repo to the `brew`-upgraded binary" path needs no network. **Memory reconciliation** (this hydrate): rewrote the `#### fab upgrade-repo` step 1 to the precedence list + offline-first rationale, updated its scenarios, added the Design Decision above. Rate-limit error-message naming and `GH_TOKEN` reading deferred as follow-ups (valuable only on the `--latest` path). No migration (binary behavior + docs only). `fab-setup.md` / `SPEC-fab-setup.md` confirmed unaffected. (1hmj)
- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — feat: Per-Stage Model Selection via Named Tiers (#406)
- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — Added migration `2.2.0-to-2.3.0.md` — surfaces the new optional `agent.tiers` per-stage-model override to existing users by inserting a **fully-commented** `agent.tiers` reference block under the `agent:` block in `fab/project/config.yaml` (the fixed stage→tier mapping reference, fab-kit's built-in default profiles, and the override shape — all commented out; fab-kit uses its built-in defaults until a user uncomments a tier). Config-only and additive: **no `.status.yaml` schema change**; configs without `agent.tiers` are fully supported. Pre-check skips when `config.yaml` is absent or when a `tiers:` key / the reference-comment sentinel `# agent.tiers — per-stage model` is already present (the sentinel gives idempotency for an all-commented block with no parseable yaml key — same technique as `0.34.0-to-0.37.0.md`'s `linear_workspace` commented field). All other keys (`project`, `source_paths`, `checklist`, `fab_version`, `agent.spawn_command`, `true_impact_exclude`, `test_paths`, `stage_hooks`, `branch_prefix`) preserved. Naming follows the j6cs/c5tr precedent (FROM = current release `2.2.0`, TO = next minor `2.3.0`); range non-overlap with `2.1.6-to-2.2.0.md` verified (its TO = this FROM). `src/kit/VERSION` left at `2.2.0` — the bump is release-owned. See [_shared/configuration.md](/_shared/configuration.md) for the `agent.tiers` schema. (l3ja)

## 2026-06-12
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — test: Tests, CI & Toolchain (#403)

- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Hardened the auto-download path and made the lifecycle exit codes truthful (findings F16–F22 of `docs/specs/findings/binary-review-2026-06-12.md` §B3, plus absorbed backlog `[1old]`). **Download** (`src/go/fab-kit/internal/download.go` + new `lock.go`): dedicated HTTP clients (flat 30s for `LatestVersion`/sums; 30s dial/TLS/`ResponseHeaderTimeout` + 10-minute context deadline for the archive stream — no more `http.Get`/`http.DefaultClient`), version-keyed blocking `syscall.Flock` on `versions/<v>.lock` with post-acquire `ResolveBinary` re-check, hash-then-extract verification against a same-release `SHA256SUMS` asset (404 → warn-and-skip; mismatch/missing entry → refuse), extraction into `versions/<v>.tmp-<pid>` + atomic rename (readiness now all-or-nothing; stale binary-less dirs replaced under the lock), error cleanup scoped to temp artifacts. **Release workflow**: new `Generate SHA256SUMS` step + `dist/SHA256SUMS` in the `gh release create` asset list (only edits — test steps are `tb6f`'s). **upgrade-repo** (F18): sync runs FIRST and `fab_version` is stamped only after success — sync failure exits non-zero with "run 'fab sync' to repair, then re-run 'fab upgrade-repo'", no `Updated:` line, and re-runs retry (the `:116-130` documented contract is now enforced). **fab update** (F19): not-brew path returns `ErrNotBrewInstalled` (non-zero exit, guidance preserved). **sync** (F21): deployment/scaffolding write failures are counted (`failed N` tally), surfaced per-skill on stderr, and exit non-zero. **Version threading** (F22): `Init(version)`/`Upgrade(version, target)` pass the embedded binary version into `Sync(systemVersion, kitVersion, …)`. **fab init** ([1old]): git-repo precondition before any download/config write. Memory reconciliation: new Auto-Download Hardening subsection; init/upgrade-repo step lists rewritten (also dropping the pre-existing stale "copy kit/ into the repo's `src/kit/`" steps — kit content has been cache-served since `260402-gnx5`); Atomic Update section repointed at the cache install; verified incidental corrections to the release asset list (4 `kit-*` + 4 `brew-*`, no generic `kit.tar.gz`). `_cli-fab.md` gained a "Workspace Command Exit Semantics" table (constitution-mandated). versionGuard post-state semantics documented in [kit-architecture.md](/distribution/kit-architecture.md). (dn2c)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — **Release workflow test gate** (binary-review B6, F40/F41/F42): `release.yml` now runs `just test` (both Go modules) immediately after checkout — before the manual-dispatch tag mint and before any build/package/release step — so a red suite ships nothing and never even creates the tag; previously binaries shipped to Homebrew users on any `v*` tag push with zero test steps. The hardcoded `go-version: '1.22'` is replaced with `go-version-file: src/go/fab/go.mod`, making ci.yml's "single source of truth" comment true — the Go version now lives only in the module go.mod files (bumped to 1.26 in this change). ci.yml additionally runs both module suites with `-race` and cross-compiles darwin/arm64 (build + vet) on both matrix legs, so the build-constrained `*_darwin.go` files are type-checked on every PR instead of first compiling inside the release workflow after the tag is pushed. Release-workflow step list updated (setup-go step rewritten, `just test` inserted as step 4). Toolchain bump + yaml.v3 pin decision + golden byte-stability suite in [kit-architecture.md](/distribution/kit-architecture.md). (tb6f)

- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Enforced the fab-kit lifecycle contracts this file documents (findings F16–F22 + absorbed `[1old]`; sibling entry in [distribution.md](/distribution/distribution.md) covers the download/release side). `Sync` signature is now `Sync(systemVersion, kitVersion string, shimOnly, projectOnly bool)` — `Init(systemVersion)`/`Upgrade(systemVersion, targetVersion)` receive the embedded binary version from `cmd/fab-kit/main.go` and pass the kit version explicitly (`var runSync = Sync` added as their test seam). `versionGuard` rewritten to post-state verification (new Design Decision above): attempts `Update()`, re-checks `installedBinaryVersion()` (new package-var seam running `fab-kit --version` from PATH), always fails the current run when tripped; `Update()` returns new sentinel `ErrNotBrewInstalled` on the not-brew path. Deployment writes fail loudly: `syncAgentSkills` counts per-skill failures (`WARN:` stderr lines + `failed N` tally) and returns an error, `deploySkills` joins per-agent errors, `scaffoldDirectories` propagates writes (incl. `.kit-migration-version` + kit `VERSION` read), `Sync` returns non-nil (no `Done.`) on any of them. `Upgrade` runs sync before stamping `fab_version` (stamp-after-success; non-zero exit + repair guidance on failure). `Init` gains the git-repo precondition before any download/config write. Cache installs documented as atomic (temp-dir + rename under flock; exec-bit no longer a readiness proxy). Updated here: fab-kit subcommand bullets (incl. correcting the stale `fab-kit upgrade` name to `upgrade-repo`), the 6-step sync paragraph, the "Updating `.kit/`" section (dropping the stale "atomically replaces `src/kit/`" copy-into-repo phrasing — cache-served since `260402-gnx5`), the fab-go Binary-location paragraph, and the new Design Decision. Tests: `upgrade_test.go` created; `lock_test.go`, download/update/sync/init suites extended. `_cli-fab.md` gained the Workspace Command Exit Semantics table. (dn2c)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Scanner-truncation sweep (binary-review batch B2/6). New `internal/lines` package (`ReadFileLines`/`Split`, per-line trailing-`\r` TrimSuffix for CRLF parity) replaced every unchecked production `bufio.Scanner` site — score `countGrades` (content-based per mz4q F02, lines via `Split`), archive `removeFromIndex`, backlog `ParsePending`/`ExtractContent`, hooklib `HasSectionHeading`/`scanSectionItems` (via `Split`), prmeta checkbox counters, frontmatter `Field`/`HasFrontmatter`, memoryindex `readH1` — leaving `internal/proc/proc_linux.go` (the one `Err()`-checking site) as the sole production scanner. New `internal/atomicfile` package (`WriteFile(path, data, perm)`: temp+fsync+chmod+rename, cleanup on failure) serving the archive-index writers; it mirrors the `statusfile.Save` pattern but `statusfile.Save`/`runtime.SaveFile` keep their own inline implementations — mz4q F03/F04 gave them deliberately distinct fsync postures (statusfile fsyncs, runtime skips fsync on the hook hot path), so a single always-fsync helper cannot serve both. `batch_archive.go`'s `hydrateStatusRe` regex deleted — `isArchivable` rewritten on `statusfile.Load` + `GetProgress("hydrate")`, so statusfile's single `.status.yaml` ownership holds everywhere (fixtures rewritten as realistic `progress:` blocks). Error-return signature changes: `ParsePending`, `ExtractContent` (accurate errors), `removeFromIndex`, `updateIndex` (3-value, content passed to `backfillIndex` — no post-write re-read); `Compute`/`ComputeWithStatus` stop swallowing persistence failures (`persist confidence:` / `log confidence:` hard errors for explicit `fab score`; the hook keeps its `err == nil` guard). Architecture + Testing sections updated (`internal/lines`, `internal/atomicfile` added); the `changetypes_doc_test.go` scanner note re-scoped to test-only. `_cli-fab.md` fab-score failure rows updated same-PR. (hv7t)

## 2026-06-11
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — Fixed the bootstrap trigger that never fired on the canonical install path (f024): step 1a broadened from "config.yaml doesn't exist" to "missing OR raw template OR missing required fields `project.name`/`project.description`" — `fab init` writes a `fab_version`-only config.yaml, so the old trigger was always false after `fab init` → `/fab-setup` and project configuration was silently skipped. The Config Pre-flight create-mode definition uses the same three-part condition. Config Create Mode now explicitly **preserves an existing `fab_version` key** when writing from the scaffold template (the scaffold lacks it; the fab router errors without it). Skill + `SPEC-fab-setup.md` updated. (9u91)

- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — Moved migration **discovery** into the `fab-kit` binary (`src/go/fab-kit/internal/migrations.go`: `parseMigrationFilename`, `DiscoverMigrations` → `DiscoverResult{Local,Engine,Applicable,GapSkips,Overlaps}`; reuses `parseSemver`/`compareSemver` from `sync.go` (now `semver.go` since the tb6f split), no new dep) and exposed it as the queryable command `fab migrations-status [--json]` (registered in the router's `fabKitArgs` allowlist; exit 0 on clean query incl. overlap, non-zero only on genuine error; read-only). `fab upgrade-repo` now mechanically detects relevance via `DiscoverMigrations` instead of string inequality: overlap → warn + don't stamp; applicable → TTY-gated bold+yellow reminder + don't stamp; empty/no-op → **silently** stamp `fab/.kit-migration-version` to the target (stops drift); missing file → preserve init guidance. TTY detection is dependency-free (`os.ModeCharDevice`). `/fab-setup migrations` Step 2-3 now consume `fab migrations-status --json` (STOP on `overlaps`, else apply each `applicable` file in order); *application* of each migration file stays LLM-driven (Constitution I). No `{FROM}-to-{TO}.md` file shipped — tool-behavior change only. Companions updated: `_cli-fab.md`, `SPEC-fab-setup.md`. (9733)

- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — docs(memory): replace fab-workflow pseudo-domain with real topic domains (#381)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Conformed the repo to shll.ai's README-extraction contract for the **§9-ACTIVE era**. Four content changes: (1) removed the stale `docs/site/README.md` placeholder (it asserted "§9 RESERVED / NOT YET IMPLEMENTED" and, with `docs/site/**` now pulled, would have rendered as a live self-contradicting `/tools/fab-kit/README` page on a reserved slug); (2) removed the `docs/internal/` folder entirely (the concept was deleted from the contract on 2026-06-08 — the pull surface is now exactly `README.md` + `docs/site/**`, so maintainer notes live anywhere outside `docs/site/`); (3) added `docs/site/install.md` (→ `/tools/fab-kit/install`) and `docs/site/workflows.md` (→ `/tools/fab-kit/workflows`) as closed-set depth pages obeying the four producer rules (closure/no-`..`-escape, external links absolute-by-author, all images absolute, README→docs/site links plain-inline); (4) added two plain-inline README→docs/site body links. **Memory reconciliation** (this hydrate): updated the `#### docs/ Audience-Axis Layout` table + bullets so `docs/site/` reads "pulled + rendered one page per file (§9 ACTIVE)" and the `docs/internal/` row/bullet are gone; rewrote the `docs/site/` bullet to the closed-set-tree model; updated the frontmatter `description:` tail; added the Design Decision above. **Supersedes** the §9-RESERVED deviation recorded by `260605-fcrp` (the contract flipped RESERVED→ACTIVE in `x0br`, 2026-06-07); the `260605-fcrp` Design-Decision `docs/site/` clause is annotated as superseded for history. Producer-side, this-repo-only `docs` change — no shll.ai edits, no CLI/skill changes. (yfg8)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — docs(memory): replace fab-workflow pseudo-domain with real topic domains (#381)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Relocated the operator state file from repo-rooted `gitRepoRoot()/.fab-operator.yaml` to a **server-keyed XDG path** `<stateDir>/fab/operator/<server-slug>.yaml`. Added the helper quartet to `cmd/fab/operator.go`: `stateDir()` (XDG base — `$XDG_STATE_HOME` if set+absolute, else `$HOME/.local/state`; uniform Linux/macOS, never `~/Library`), `slugify()` (strip leading separator, separators → `-`; deterministic, collision-free, fs-safe; empty → `"default"`), `serverSlug()` (slugifies the tmux `#{socket_path}` via `pane.WithServer`; `"default"` fallback when tmux can't be queried), and `StatePath()` (`MkdirAll`'d parent, `tick-start` calls `StatePath("")`). Keyed on the socket path (survives a tmux-server restart) not the server PID (would orphan the file). `runOperatorTickStart` rewired to `StatePath(server)`; test seam renamed `operatorRepoRootOverride` → `operatorStatePathOverride` (now a full file path). No migration of old files — abandoned in place. `runOperator` launch CWD unchanged (still `gitRepoRoot()`). Added `fab spawn-command [--repo <path>]` (`cmd/fab/spawn_command.go`, wired in `main.go`) — prints a repo's `agent.spawn_command` via `spawn.Command(configPath)`, reading `<path>/fab/project/config.yaml` for `--repo` else `resolve.FabRoot()`; `spawn.DefaultSpawnCommand` fallback. Added `spawn-command` to the `internal/spawn` consumer list. Also fixed `fab pane map` to compute `mainRoot` per-repo and expose a `repo` JSON field (documented in [pane-commands.md](/runtime/pane-commands.md)). `_cli-fab.md` updated (constitution-mandated). (h3jk)

- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — docs(memory): replace fab-workflow pseudo-domain with real topic domains (#381)

## 2026-06-07
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Extended `fab memory-index` to **recurse one level into sub-domains** (resolving the PR #377 Copilot finding that `gatherFiles` was depth-2-only): `DomainData` gains a `SubDomains []DomainData` field; `gatherSubDomains` enumerates each domain's child dirs with ≥1 non-index `.md` and builds a `DomainData` per sub-domain via the existing `gatherFiles`; `RenderDomain` appends a `## Sub-Domains` table only when `len(SubDomains) > 0` (sub-domain-free output byte-identical) and also renders each `{domain}/{sub-domain}/index.md` (no `RenderSubDomain` — tier-agnostic file-row contract); `cmd/fab/memory_index.go` flattens domains + sub-domains into the write/check target list. Width/depth warnings now fire across the recursive tree (over-wide sub-domain warns; width exemption is domain-tier only). New `internal/memoryindex`/`cmd/fab` tests for nested-tree render, sub-domain discovery/ordering, idempotency, depth-4 warning, empty-sub-dir, and the cmd-level sub-domain index generation. Updated the `fab memory-index` subcommand description and the `internal/memoryindex` Architecture paragraph. The `tciy` "Generated Memory Index" design decision's deferred-follow-up note flipped to "shipped in `sx7a`" (apply path active; External addressing). (sx7a)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added the `fab memory-index [--check]` subcommand (`cmd/fab/memory_index.go` + `internal/memoryindex/`) to the fab-go subcommand list — deterministically regenerates the `docs/memory/` index files (root = domains-only; domain = `| File | Description | Last Updated |`) from folder contents + `description:` frontmatter + `git log` dates, byte-stable/idempotent, with non-fatal shape-bound stderr warnings (~12 width / depth 3; `_shared`/`_unsorted` width-exempt) and a `--check` staleness guard. Documented `internal/memoryindex` in the Architecture paragraph (prmeta-style RenderRoot/RenderDomain + Gather split; `filepath.Dir(resolve.FabRoot())` repo-root; domain-description round-trip through frontmatter) and added it to the Testing tested-packages list. New design decision "Generated Memory Index via Deterministic Go (`fab memory-index`)" with the deferred file-moving rebalancer follow-up (`sx7a`, an agent-driven enhancement to `docs-reorg-memory`). (tciy)

## 2026-06-05
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Added the **shll.ai Public Docs Site (README-Slice Pull)** Requirements subsection: documented the second pull surface (the README slice rendered at `/tools/fab-kit/readme` by `ReadmeSlice.astro`, pulled into `content/fab-kit/README.md` by `scheduled-readme-refresh.yml`; tool repo canonical, shll.ai never hand-edits) alongside the existing `help-dump` command-reference pull. Captured the producer-side conformance obligation now satisfied in `README.md`: (1) tail boundary at a single top-level `## Development` heading — Stage Coverage matrix + Companion tools + Learn More moved below it as `###` (GitHub-only); (2) both ` ```mermaid ` fences kept, each followed by an absolute `raw.githubusercontent.com/.../docs/img/<name>.svg` image (hand-authored `pipeline-stages.svg` + `stage-coverage.svg`, no build/CI/render step — Constitution I); (3) ~13 repo-relative `docs/specs/*.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` links in the slice region rewritten to absolute `github.com/.../blob/main/...` URLs; broken `#standalone-cli-tools` anchors dropped. Added the `docs/` audience-axis table: new `docs/internal/` (maintainer notes, never reaches site; distinct from `docs/specs/`/`docs/memory/`) and `docs/img/` (SVG assets). **Deviation recorded**: `docs/site/` landed as **structure + explainer README only — no content migrated**, because shll.ai's §9 `docs/site/` pull path is RESERVED/UNIMPLEMENTED (puller fetches only `README.md`); migrating load-bearing prose would strand it on both surfaces. Producer-side, this-repo-only `docs` change — no shll.ai edits, no `extract-readme.ts` changes, no CLI/skill changes. Folded into `distribution.md` rather than a new `conventions/` domain (thin/orphaned single-file domain avoided; `distribution.md` already owns the fab-kit↔shll.ai relationship). (fcrp)

## 2026-06-03
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Tore down the deprecated push-side shll.ai integration now that shll.ai's puller is live and proven. Removed the entire `Help-dump → shll.ai` step from `.github/workflows/release.yml` (CI step list 7→5 entries) — both the fatal `fab help-dump > help/fab-kit.json` + `jq` dump/validate self-check AND the auto-merging cross-repo PR transport into `sahil87/shll.ai` (clone-with-token + `help-dump/fab-kit-<version>` branch + `gh pr create` + `gh pr merge --auto --squash`, secret `SHLLAI_TOKEN`). `Build all targets` now flows directly into `Package kit archives`. Removed the now-dead `/help/` entry (and its transient-artifact comment) from `.gitignore`. The `help-dump` command (`src/go/fab/cmd/fab/helpdump.go`, `helpdump_test.go`) and `src/kit/skills/_cli-fab.md` were deliberately left untouched — `help-dump` is the contract surface shll.ai's now-live puller invokes (it pulls the help tree itself rather than fab-kit pushing it). Manual follow-up (out-of-band, not a file edit): delete the `SHLLAI_TOKEN` repo secret from fab-kit's GitHub settings — a repo-wide grep confirmed it was referenced only by the removed step. Reverts the push-side CI mechanics added by `260602-xob7` while preserving the producer command. (mtf9)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Release workflow gained a `Help-dump → shll.ai` step after `Build all targets` (CI step list 5→7 entries). The step runs `./dist/bin/fab-go-linux-amd64 help-dump > help/fab-kit.json`, validates it with `jq -e '.tool=="fab" and .schema_version==1 and (.version|length>0) and (.root|type=="object")'` (fatal), then opens an auto-merging PR into the external repo `sahil87/shll.ai` (clone-with-token + branch `help-dump/fab-kit-<version>` + `gh pr create` + `gh pr merge --auto --squash`, secret `SHLLAI_TOKEN`) writing `help/fab-kit.json` — non-fatal and idempotent (skips on byte-identical content via `git diff --cached --quiet`). PR-not-direct-push avoids the 7-tool push race into the single shll.ai repo. Dumps the rich `fab-go` binary (carries the command tree + `main.version` ldflags), not the shim. `/help/` added to `.gitignore` (the JSON is a transient shll.ai-owned artifact, not retained by fab-kit). shll.ai's site-side consumer (Astro loader + UI) is tracked separately in the shll.ai repo. (xob7)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — `internal/score` gains a code↔doc consistency test (`changetypes_doc_test.go`, `TestDocTablesMatchScoringMaps`): the `expectedMin`/`gateThresholds` maps in `score.go` stay canonical, and the test parses the "Expected Minimum Decisions" and "Gate Thresholds" tables in `docs/specs/change-types.md`, asserting they match the resolved `getExpectedMin`/`getGateThreshold` for all 7 change types plus a bidirectional exact-type-set coverage check. Parser reuses the package's `bufio.Scanner`/pipe-split idiom (no markdown library); `findDocFile` walks up from the test CWD to the repo root. No map values changed — drift now fails `just test`/CI. Added to the Testing section's `internal/score` description. (s6q5)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added a hidden, CI/build-time-only `fab help-dump` subcommand to the rich `fab-go` CLI (`src/go/fab/cmd/fab/helpdump.go` — `helpDumpCmd()`, `dumpDoc`, recursive `buildNode`; registered in `main.go`'s `root.AddCommand`). It serializes the live cobra command tree to the frozen shll.ai contract JSON on stdout (`{tool:"fab", version, captured_at, schema_version:1, root:Node}`), filtering `completion`/`help`/`Hidden` at every level, sorting children by `Name()`, emitting `commands:[]` for leaves, and using `SetEscapeHTML(false)` + 2-space indent. Marked `Hidden: true`, so it is absent from `fab --help` and from its own dumped tree. Test: `src/go/fab/cmd/fab/helpdump_test.go`. Consumed by the new `Help-dump → shll.ai` release-workflow step (see [distribution.md](/distribution/distribution.md)). (xob7)

## 2026-06-01
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Removed `templates/spec.md` from the kit tree (its requirement discipline absorbed into `plan.md`'s `## Requirements`); updated the `plan.md` tree note to "unified `## Requirements` + `## Tasks` + `## Acceptance`" and the `status.yaml` note to "6-stage progress". Go touch points (verified): `internal/statusfile` `StageOrder` dropped `spec` (length 6; `StageNumber("apply")==2`) and `encodeConfidence` stopped writing `indicative` (decode-tolerant `Indicative *bool` retained); `internal/status` `validateStage` added a `spec`-deprecation branch mirroring `tasks`, and `SetConfidence`/`SetConfidenceFuzzy` dropped their `indicative` param; `internal/score` collapsed `expectedMinIntake`/`expectedMinSpec` into one `expectedMin` map and set `gateThresholds` flat 3.0, repointing `Compute`/`CheckGate` to `intake.md`; `internal/hooklib/artifact.go` `MatchArtifactPath` dropped `spec.md` and `cmd/fab/hook.go` removed its `case "spec.md"` branch in lockstep; `internal/change` `fab change list` row dropped the `:indicative` 5th field (→ `name:display_stage:display_state:score`). Shipped migration `1.9.7-to-1.10.0.md` + `src/kit/VERSION` bump to `1.10.0`. (j6cs)
- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — Added migration `1.9.7-to-1.10.0.md` for the spec-stage collapse (the direct sibling of `1.8.0-to-1.9.0.md`, which merged `tasks` into `apply`). `src/kit/VERSION` bumped to `1.10.0`. Walks every in-flight change folder under `fab/changes/` (excluding `archive/**`); idempotent and archive-safe. Per change: (1) rewrite `.status.yaml` to drop `progress.spec`, folding its activity level into `apply` (done/skipped → leave apply; active/ready → carry the level only if apply is still `pending`; pending/absent → leave apply); (2) **four-state spec.md→plan.md case table** — *spec.md only* → leave for on-apply ingestion, do NOT create a `plan.md` stub (a stub would trip the resumability skip-guard and deadlock plan generation); *plan.md only* → progress rewrite only; *both* → merge the `spec.md` requirement body into `plan.md`'s `## Requirements` (annotated `<!-- migrated from spec.md on {date} -->`, leaving `spec.md` with a "safe to delete" comment, NOT deleting it); *neither* → progress rewrite only. Idempotency sentinel: skip the merge if `plan.md` already has a `## Requirements` heading or the migration marker. Project-wide: relocate `stage_directives.spec` directives into `stage_directives.apply` (de-duplicated, order-preserving) rather than dropping them. Leaves any `confidence.indicative` key on disk untouched (the 1.10.0 binary tolerates it on read). Re-run is a complete no-op. (j6cs)

## 2026-05-12
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Router Architecture section rewritten to describe the always-route policy. Removed all references to the `fabGoNoConfigArgs` allowlist (no longer exists in `src/go/fab-kit/cmd/fab/main.go`) and the "Not in a fab-managed repo. Run 'fab init' to set one up." exit (gone). Router now routes every non-fab-kit command to `fab-go` regardless of `config.yaml` presence; version selection is inline (project-pinned when `cfg != nil`, router-bundled otherwise). fab-go's per-command guards are the authoritative gate. Corrupted-config path (parse error) still hard-errors. The "Not in a fab-managed repo, workflow command" scenario rewritten to reflect that workflow commands now reach fab-go and either run (config-free) or fail-closed via fab-go's own guard. (c432)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Router config gate removed entirely. Deleted `fabGoNoConfigArgs` allowlist and the `resolveFabVersion` helper from `src/go/fab-kit/cmd/fab/main.go`; the "Not in a fab-managed repo. Run 'fab init' to set one up." exit is gone. Router now applies an always-route policy: every non-fab-kit command dispatches to `fab-go` regardless of `config.yaml` presence. Version selection is inline in `execFabGo` — project-pinned when `cfg != nil`, router-bundled (`-ldflags -X` build-time `version`) otherwise. fab-go's per-command guards (typically `resolve.FabRoot()`) are now the sole authority on whether a command needs project state. Corrupted-config path (parse error) still hard-errors at the router — only missing-config becomes a soft fall-through. New `fab shell-init <shell>` subcommand added to fab-go (`src/go/fab/cmd/fab/shellinit.go`, factory `shellInitCmd()`, registered via `root.AddCommand`) as a `tu`-style alias for Cobra's auto-generated `completion <shell>`; delegates to `cmd.Root().GenBashCompletion / GenZshCompletion / GenFishCompletion`. Recommended install: `eval "$(fab shell-init zsh)"`. Unblocks sourcing shell completion from `~/.zshrc` outside a fab repo. Removed router tests: `TestFabGoNoConfigArgs`, `TestResolveFabVersion`. README updated with the `eval` one-liner. (c432)

## 2026-05-06
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — `wt` and `idea` split out of fab-kit's brew tarball into standalone Homebrew formulas in `sahil87/tap` (formerly bundled). fab-kit's formula declares them as `depends_on "sahil87/tap/wt"` and `depends_on "sahil87/tap/idea"`. Brew tarball shrinks from 4 binaries to 2 (`fab`, `fab-kit`); cross-compile matrix shrinks from 5×4=20 to 3×4=12. `src/go/wt/` and `src/go/idea/` removed; canonical sources are `github.com/sahil87/wt` and `github.com/sahil87/idea`. `link_overwrite "bin/wt"` / `link_overwrite "bin/idea"` in the standalone formulas handle the upgrade-conflict from `fab-kit 1.6.2` (which owned those symlinks) transparently. Release notes for `1.7.0` document the `brew unlink wt idea && brew upgrade fab-kit` fallback for the rare case `link_overwrite` does not resolve cleanly. First release carrying this change is `1.7.0` (semver minor — user-visible install set unchanged via `depends_on` transitivity). Also swept stale `wvrdz/tap` references in active prose to `sahil87/tap` (historical Changelog rows preserved). (4rtx)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Swept stale `brew tap wvrdz/tap` reference in the live bootstrap example to `brew tap sahil87/tap` (residual from the `260401-ixzv` org migration). The historical Changelog row at the bottom of this file preserves the original `wvrdz` mention. No structural change to `src/kit/` architecture. (4rtx)
- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — Swept stale `brew tap wvrdz/tap` instruction at the brew-install migration's prerequisite gate to `brew tap sahil87/tap` (residual from the `260401-ixzv` org migration). No structural change to the migration system itself. (4rtx)
- **Update** [migrations](/distribution/migrations.md) — Added migration `1.8.0-to-1.9.0.md` for the tasks-stage collapse and `plan.md` schema. Per change folder under `fab/changes/` (excluding `archive/`): (1) idempotent no-op when `plan.md` already exists; (2) when only legacy `tasks.md` and/or `checklist.md` are present, produce `plan.md` by concatenating bodies under `## Tasks` and `## Acceptance` headings (verbatim modulo the legacy file's H1 + frontmatter — subheadings, `T-NNN`/`CHK-NNN` IDs, `[P]` markers, body content all preserved); one-sided legacy state writes a placeholder + warning rather than failing; (3) rewrite `.status.yaml` — drop `progress.tasks` (preserve `progress.apply` if `tasks: done|skipped`; advance to `tasks`'s state if `active|ready`), replace `checklist:` block with `plan:` block (`generated: true`, `task_count` = count under `## Tasks`, `acceptance_count` = old `checklist.total`, `acceptance_completed` = old `checklist.completed`); (4) append `<!-- Migrated to plan.md on {DATE} — safe to delete. -->` to legacy files (do NOT delete); (5) prune `stage_directives.tasks` from user `config.yaml`. Archived changes under `fab/changes/archive/**` are untouched. `src/kit/VERSION` bumped to `1.9.0`. (qszh)

- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Replaced root-level `fab pane-map` with `fab pane` parent command grouping four subcommands: `map` (renamed from `pane-map`, identical behavior), `capture` (structured pane content capture with fab context enrichment, `--json`/`--raw` output modes), `send` (safe send-keys with pane existence and agent idle validation, `--force` override), `process` (OS-level process tree detection via `/proc` on Linux and `ps` on macOS, with process classification and `has_agent` detection). Extracted shared pane resolution logic from `panemap.go` into new `internal/pane/` package (`ValidatePane`, `ResolvePaneContext`, `GetPanePID`, `FindMainWorktreeRoot`). Updated `_cli-fab.md` (new `## fab pane` section replacing `## fab pane-map`). Reduced `_cli-external.md` tmux section (`capture-pane` and `send-keys` removed, `new-window` retained). Platform-specific process discovery via Go build tags (`//go:build linux` / `//go:build darwin`). (tam1)

## 2026-04-02
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Removed `fab/.kit-sync-version` from preserved files list. Sync staleness detection now compares `$(fab kit-path)/VERSION` against `fab_version` in `config.yaml` (single warning message). (0ak9)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Three-binary architecture: Homebrew formula installs 4 binaries (`fab`, `fab-kit`, `wt`, `idea`). Shim section renamed to "Router Architecture" — `fab` uses negative-match dispatch to `fab-kit` or `fab-go`. Build produces 5 binaries (20 cross-compiled). Binary table updated: `fab` (router) from `src/go/fab-kit/cmd/fab/`, `fab-kit` from `src/go/fab-kit/cmd/fab-kit/`. `fab-sync.sh` references replaced with `fab-kit sync` / `fab sync`. `init` and `upgrade` call `Sync()` directly instead of `fab-sync.sh`. Updated design decisions (Homebrew distribution, fab upgrade). (3ac3)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Homebrew distribution model: system `fab` shim installed via `brew install fab-kit` (formula at `wvrdz/homebrew-tap`). Shim reads `fab_version` from `config.yaml`, resolves cached `fab-go` at `~/.fab-kit/versions/`, auto-fetches on miss. `fab init` bootstraps new repos (primary method replaces curl one-liner). `fab upgrade` replaces `fab-upgrade.sh` (shim subcommand). `wt` and `idea` become system-only Homebrew binaries. `fab-go binary at ` emptied (binary-free repo). Backend override mechanism (`FAB_BACKEND`, `.fab-backend`) removed. Sync pipeline: `4-get-fab-binary.sh` removed, `5-sync-hooks.sh` calls `fab hook sync` (system shim), `.envrc` scaffold removes `PATH_add src/kit/bin`. Release archives restructured for shim cache extraction (`fab-go` + `kit/` content). 4 Go binaries: `fab` (shim, Homebrew), `fab-go` (per-version cache), `wt` (Homebrew), `idea` (Homebrew). (46hw)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Removed `scaffold/.github/copilot-code-review.yml` from the scaffold tree. Cleaned stale entries from `scaffold/fragment-.gitignore` (`fab/changes/**/.pr-done`, `/.ralph`). Scaffold file count unchanged at 11 (3 fragment, 8 copy-if-absent) after removal of the Copilot config and stale `.gitignore` lines. (5tci)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Kit content no longer copied to user projects — served entirely from system cache at `~/.fab-kit/versions/<version>/kit/`. `fab init` and `fab upgrade` no longer create `fab/.kit/` in projects. Source repo layout: `fab/.kit/` renamed to `src/kit/`. Build scripts (`justfile`, `release.sh`) updated to read from `src/kit/`. `.gitignore` cleaned of `fab/.kit/` entries. `fab kit-path` command added for agent-agnostic kit path resolution. Bootstrap one-liner and manual copy references updated. (gnx5)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Migrated GitHub org references from wvrdz to sahil87. License changed from PolyForm Internal Use to MIT (root LICENSE). (ixzv)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Rewrote `fab-kit sync` to resolve kit content from system cache (`~/.fab-kit/versions/{version}/kit/`) instead of `src/kit/` in the repo. 6-step pipeline: prerequisites, version guard, ensure cache, scaffolding, direnv, project scripts. Added `--shim` (steps 1-5) and `--project` (step 6) flags. Absorbed hook sync into step 4 (replicated hooklib in `fab-kit` internal package). Removed `5-sync-hooks.sh`. Fixed `fragment-.envrc` (`fab-kit sync` -> `fab sync`). Updated prerequisites (removed jq, gh — no longer needed by sync). Updated release archive description (sync/ now empty). (ktbg)

- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Three-binary architecture: split shim into `fab` (router) and `fab-kit` (workspace lifecycle), alongside `fab-go` (workflow engine). Source at `src/go/fab-kit/` with two `cmd/` entries sharing `internal/`. `fab-kit sync` replaces `fab-sync.sh` and `sync/{1,2,3}-*.sh` (clean cut removal). Router uses negative-match dispatch (fab-kit commands allowlisted, everything else to fab-go). `fab help` composes output from both sub-binaries. Added 5 design decisions (three-binary split, negative-match routing, single Go module, clean cut sync migration, 5-sync-hooks retained). Updated directory tree, agent deployment, bootstrap, updating, and portability sections. (3ac3)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Migrated all 6 shell scripts and 2 lib files from `src/kit/scripts/` to Go binary subcommands. `fab-doctor.sh` → `fab doctor` (`fab-kit` subcommand, added to router allowlist). `fab-help.sh` → `fab fab-help` (`fab-go` subcommand with dynamic skill scanning via `internal/frontmatter/`). `fab-operator.sh` → `fab operator` (`fab-go` subcommand). 3 batch scripts → `fab batch new|switch|archive` (`fab-go` subcommand group). `lib/spawn.sh` → `internal/spawn/` package. `lib/frontmatter.sh` → `internal/frontmatter/` package. Deleted entire `src/kit/scripts/` directory. Updated skills: `/fab-setup` (doctor path), `/fab-help` (script path), `/fab-operator` (launcher path). Updated `_cli-fab.md` with new command signatures. (41gc)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Binary-free `.kit/`: removed `fab`, `fab-go`, `wt`, `idea` from `fab-go binary at ` (only `.gitkeep` remains). System `fab` shim (Homebrew) dispatches to cached `fab-go` at `~/.fab-kit/versions/`. `wt` and `idea` are system-only Homebrew binaries. Removed shell dispatcher, backend override mechanism (`FAB_BACKEND`, `.fab-backend`). Removed `fab-upgrade.sh` (replaced by `fab upgrade` shim subcommand). Removed `4-get-fab-binary.sh` from sync pipeline. Updated `5-sync-hooks.sh` to call `fab hook sync` via system shim. Updated `fab-doctor.sh` to check for `fab` system binary. - **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Kit content relocated from `fab/.kit/` (in-project) to `~/.fab-kit/versions/<version>/kit/` (system cache). Hook shell scripts eliminated — hooks now inline as `fab hook <subcommand>` in settings.local.json. `kit.conf` eliminated (build-type removed, repo hardcoded). Source repo layout: `fab/.kit/` renamed to `src/kit/`. Skills reference templates via `$(fab kit-path)/templates/`. Build scripts updated (`justfile`, `release.sh`, `.gitignore`). Added `fab runtime set-idle` and `fab runtime clear-idle` Go subcommands replacing yq in hooks. Updated `on-stop.sh` and `on-session-start.sh` descriptions (yq → fab runtime). Updated `5-sync-hooks.sh` to support tool-name matchers for PostToolUse events. Added `runtime` package to Go binary architecture. (6bba)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Deleted 20 orphaned shell test files (`src/lib/*/test.bats`, `src/lib/*/SPEC-*.md`, `src/lib/*/test-simple.sh`, `src/lib/calc-score/sensitivity.sh`, `src/sync/test-5-sync-hooks.bats`) and removed `src/lib/` and `src/sync/` directories. Removed "Dev folder" references from statusman, logman, calc-score, and archiveman sections. Added Go parity test documentation note. Added 4 missing status subcommands (`add-issue`, `get-issues`, `add-pr`, `get-prs`) to `_scripts.md`. Fixed `git-pr.md` Step 4 to pass `<change>` instead of `<status_file>` path to `add-pr`. (7arg)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added `fab pane-map` subcommand to Go binary — discovers all tmux panes, resolves worktree roots via git, reads `.fab-status.yaml` symlink for active change, correlates `.fab-runtime.yaml` for agent idle state, and renders an aligned table (Pane, Worktree, Change, Stage, Agent). Requires tmux at runtime (graceful error if not in session). Main worktree shown as `(main)`. (Later updated: 260312-kvng expanded to show all panes, not just fab worktrees.) (bh45)
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — Replaced template-driven language detection (step 1b-lang) with agent-inferred conventions. Deleted `$(fab kit-path)/templates/constitutions/` and `$(fab kit-path)/templates/configs/` (6 template files). Removed language template advisory (section 2b) from `2-sync-workspace.sh`. Added "Agent-Inferred Conventions Replace Templates" design decision. (143f)
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — Full removal of Phase 1b-lang (language detection and convention inference) from bootstrap flow — fab-kit stays language-neutral. Supersedes 260306-143f agent-inferred conventions. Added deprecated requirement for agent-inferred conventions. Updated "Agent-Inferred Conventions" design decision as superseded. Updated hook registration delegation table to reflect matcher support. (6bba)

## 2026-03-05
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Go binary (`src/go/fab/`) built — ports all lib/ scripts to single `fab` binary. No distribution changes in this change — binary inclusion in kit.tar.gz and per-platform archives are deferred to a future change. Go toolchain required only for building from source, not for end users. (bhd6)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Added `$(fab kit-path)/hooks/` as a new distributed directory (hook scripts shipped with kit). Updated bootstrap description to mention hook registration via `5-sync-hooks.sh`. Updated release archive contents to note hooks and sync scripts alongside packages. (bs5x)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Ship fab Go binary: release now produces 5 archives (generic `kit.tar.gz` + 4 per-platform `kit-{os}-{arch}.tar.gz` with Go binary at `.kit/bin/fab`). `release.sh` cross-compiles via `CGO_ENABLED=0` for darwin/arm64, darwin/amd64, linux/arm64, linux/amd64. `fab-upgrade.sh` detects platform via `uname -s`/`uname -m`, downloads platform archive with fallback to generic. README bootstrap one-liner is now platform-aware. Shell scripts in `lib/` have shim layer that delegates to Go binary when present. Skills updated via `_cli-fab.md` (renamed from `_scripts.md`) to invoke `fab-go binary at fab` as primary calling convention. (g0uq)
- **Update** [distribution](/distribution/distribution.md) — Updated generic archive (shell-only) scenario: no longer provides a working `fab` command — Go binary is required. Shell script fallback removed from dispatcher. (u8t9)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — wt-delete interactive selection menu now shows "All (N worktrees)" as first option (item 1). Selecting "All" delegates to `wt_delete_all_worktrees`. Individual worktrees shift by +1. Default selection (MRU) shifts accordingly. `--delete-all` flag preserved for non-interactive use. (38q7)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added `wt-status` to wt package — shows fab pipeline status (stage + state) per worktree. Three modes: no args (current worktree), `<name>` (specific worktree), `--all` (all worktrees). Composable architecture: atomic `wt_get_fab_status` function reads `.fab-status.yaml` symlink + `.status.yaml` via `statusman.sh display-stage`. (7zq4)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added Go binary section: `src/go/fab/` with `fab` binary porting all 7 lib/ shell scripts into cobra subcommands. Uses `internal/statusfile` as shared YAML foundation (single parse, atomic writes), eliminating yq dependency. Shell scripts unchanged — switchover is a separate change. (bhd6)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added `hooks/` directory to `.kit/` tree with `on-session-start.sh` (clears `agent` block) and `on-stop.sh` (writes `agent.idle_since` timestamp). Added `5-sync-hooks.sh` to `sync/` directory (registers hooks into `.claude/settings.local.json` via idempotent jq merge). Fixed sync directory tree listing (added missing `4-get-fab-binary.sh`, corrected sort order of `2-sync-workspace.sh` and `3-direnv.sh`). (bs5x)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Shipped Go binary: added `bin/` directory to `.kit/` tree (with `fab` binary and `.gitkeep`). Documented shim delegation layer — all 7 `lib/` shell scripts have shim at top that `exec`s `fab-go binary at fab` when present, falls through to bash when absent. Added `_scripts.md` partial as skill invocation guide — skills now call `fab-go binary at fab <command>` as primary convention. Updated bootstrap options (platform-aware + generic + manual). Updated `fab-upgrade.sh` description (platform detection via `uname`, fallback to generic). Updated `release.sh` description (cross-compiles 4 platforms, produces 5 archives). Updated Replaced list to include `bin/`. Updated "All Logic in Markdown and Shell" design decision to reflect optional Go binary with graceful degradation. (g0uq)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Added Performance Benchmark section: Rust vs Node vs optimized bash vs bash+yq for statusman.sh operations. Rust is 23-123x faster than baseline; optimized bash 2-5x; Node slower than baseline due to V8 startup (gt52)
- **Update** [kit-architecture](/distribution/kit-architecture.md) — Removed shell fallback from dispatcher (backend priority: rust > go > error, no shell fallback). Deleted all 7 ported shell scripts from `lib/` (statusman, changeman, archiveman, logman, calc-score, preflight, resolve). Only `env-packages.sh` and `frontmatter.sh` remain. Deleted `wt-status` from wt package (replaced by `fab status show`). Added `fab status show [--all] [--json] [<name>]` to Go binary for worktree pipeline status. Added `internal/worktree` package for worktree discovery. Updated `env-packages.sh` to add `$KIT_DIR/bin` to PATH. Updated `_scripts.md` to reflect Go-only backend. Updated parity tests with graceful skip when bash scripts missing. (u8t9)
- **Update** [setup](/distribution/setup.md) — Added hook registration to delegation table: `fab-sync.sh` via `5-sync-hooks.sh` registers `$(fab kit-path)/hooks/on-*.sh` into `.claude/settings.local.json` hooks (idempotent jq merge). (bs5x)
